TRAVEL_DAYLIGHT_GENERAL.rationale: A versatile travel setting that works for landscapes, monuments, and street scenes in a single exposure mode.
TRAVEL_DAYLIGHT_GENERAL.proTip: One versatile lens (24-70mm or 35mm prime) is better for street travel than multiple lenses—speed and discretion matter.
TRAVEL_DAYLIGHT_GENERAL.commonMistake: Carrying too many lenses and spending more time changing gear than shooting.

TRAVEL_CITYSCAPE.rationale: f/8 provides maximum sharpness for cityscape documentation. 1/125 is safe for handheld at wide angles.
TRAVEL_CITYSCAPE.proTip: Research the city's viewpoints before arrival—find the iconic spots and the hidden gems locals know.
TRAVEL_CITYSCAPE.commonMistake: Only shooting the tourist postcard views—find unusual angles and less-visited neighborhoods.

TRAVEL_STREET_MARKET.rationale: Markets are sensory-rich environments. Fast-ish shutter freezes vendor-customer interactions.
TRAVEL_STREET_MARKET.proTip: Spend 30 minutes observing and buying something before shooting—building rapport leads to natural, open subjects.
TRAVEL_STREET_MARKET.commonMistake: Treating subjects as props—engage genuinely before photographing to avoid cold, guarded expressions.

TRAVEL_INTERIOR_MONUMENT.rationale: Historic temple, cathedral, or palace interiors have magnificent light through high windows. IBIS critical.
TRAVEL_INTERIOR_MONUMENT.proTip: Include people for scale in monumental interiors—a single figure makes the scale of cathedrals and palaces comprehensible.
TRAVEL_INTERIOR_MONUMENT.commonMistake: Empty interior shots of famous buildings lack the scale reference that people provide.

TRAVEL_NIGHT_SCENERY.rationale: Night travel without a tripod. IBIS is essential. Fast glass and high ISO capture the authentic nocturnal city atmosphere.
TRAVEL_NIGHT_SCENERY.proTip: Night travel photography with IBIS lets you capture scenes that would require a tripod just 10 years ago.
TRAVEL_NIGHT_SCENERY.commonMistake: Not shooting at night because of the complexity—the best travel atmosphere often exists after dark.

TRAVEL_LANDSCAPE_MOUNTAIN.rationale: Mountain scenes demand maximum depth of field. Every foreground wildflower to the distant peak must be rendered sharp.
TRAVEL_LANDSCAPE_MOUNTAIN.proTip: Use a UV filter at altitude to cut through haze—mountain air has more UV scattering than sea level.
TRAVEL_LANDSCAPE_MOUNTAIN.commonMistake: Including a boring foreground—strong foreground elements (wildflowers, rocks, streams) transform mountain shots.

TRAVEL_BEACH_SCENE.rationale: High reflectivity from sand and water. Apply +0.7 EV compensation. 1/250 freezes waves and people.
TRAVEL_BEACH_SCENE.proTip: Wake up before sunrise for empty beach shots—popular beaches are typically empty at 6am.
TRAVEL_BEACH_SCENE.commonMistake: Shooting crowded beaches at peak time—arriving at dawn or dusk transforms crowded beaches into pristine ones.

TRAVEL_ARCHITECTURAL_DETAIL.rationale: Doorways, tilework, ornamental facades—travel architectural details tell cultural stories. f/8 reveals fine detail.
TRAVEL_ARCHITECTURAL_DETAIL.proTip: Look up, look down, look at what no one else is photographing—detail shots form the texture of a great travel story.
TRAVEL_ARCHITECTURAL_DETAIL.commonMistake: Only photographing the famous views and missing the extraordinary architectural details that surround them.

TRAVEL_LOCAL_PEOPLE_PORTRAIT.rationale: f/4 separates subject from background while keeping recognition context. Eye detection ensures sharp eyes.
TRAVEL_LOCAL_PEOPLE_PORTRAIT.proTip: Ask permission with a smile, show the subject their photo afterward—building a moment of connection gives a better portrait.
TRAVEL_LOCAL_PEOPLE_PORTRAIT.commonMistake: Pointing and shooting without interaction—human subjects photographed without connection often look uncomfortable.

TRAVEL_SUNRISE_SCENERY.rationale: Sunrise light is the softest, most golden light of the day. Manual mode prevents exposure drift as light changes rapidly.
TRAVEL_SUNRISE_SCENERY.proTip: Scout locations the evening before—know exactly where the sun rises and position yourself before first light.
TRAVEL_SUNRISE_SCENERY.commonMistake: Arriving at sunrise instead of before it—miss the crucial 15 minutes of pre-dawn pink sky.

TRAVEL_SUNSET_SCENERY.rationale: One of the most accessible and rewarding travel shots. Bracket exposures in RAW for maximum flexibility.
TRAVEL_SUNSET_SCENERY.proTip: Watch the sky for 15 minutes after the sun sets—the best colors often appear in the afterglow, not during sunset.
TRAVEL_SUNSET_SCENERY.commonMistake: Packing up at sunset—the best post-sunset colors arrive 10-20 minutes afterwards.

TRAVEL_STREET_NIGHT.rationale: Night travel streets are full of authentic local life. IBIS allows handheld at 1/60s for capturing the mood.
TRAVEL_STREET_NIGHT.proTip: Look for the local spots—follow the food smells and sounds to find where people actually gather at night.
TRAVEL_STREET_NIGHT.commonMistake: Staying in the tourist district at night—the authentic city comes alive after dark in neighborhoods, not tourist zones.

TRAVEL_FESTIVAL_DAYTIME.rationale: Festivals have energetic movement, color, and emotion. 1/500s freezes dancers, performers, and processional participants.
TRAVEL_FESTIVAL_DAYTIME.proTip: Research the festival schedule—know when the most photogenic elements (processions, costumes, performances) happen.
TRAVEL_FESTIVAL_DAYTIME.commonMistake: Arriving at a festival without knowing the schedule and missing the key photographic moments.

TRAVEL_BLACK_WHITE.rationale: B&W travel photography transcends time and place. Strong geometry, texture, and human interest are universal.
TRAVEL_BLACK_WHITE.proTip: B&W travel photos from 20 years ago look timeless; color travel photos can look dated. Shoot both and see which resonates.
TRAVEL_BLACK_WHITE.commonMistake: Converting snapshots to B&W—images must have strong graphic content to work in monochrome.

TRAVEL_REFLECTIONS_SCENERY.rationale: Reflections in calm water, glass facades, and polished surfaces double the visual interest of travel scenes.
TRAVEL_REFLECTIONS_SCENERY.proTip: Rain-slicked streets in cities, flooded rice paddies, and mountain lakes all create stunning reflection opportunities.
TRAVEL_REFLECTIONS_SCENERY.commonMistake: Missing reflection opportunities because you're only looking forward instead of looking down and around.
